summary: Implements v14 plan Track T1: adds scripts/cargo-deps-graph.sh (40 lines, bash -n clean) and a cargo-deps-graph CI job to each of the 3 Rust crates currently on origin/main (pheno-errors, pheno-otel, pheno-port-adapter). Each job invokes the script, produces target/deps-graph.svg, and uploads it as a GitHub Actions artifact via actions/upload-artifact@v4.
commit_message: chore(v14): T1 cargo-deps SVG per crate (L3 pillar score 1→3)

actions.rationale: Per-track T1 spec: 40-line bash wrapper that runs `cargo install cargo-deps --locked` if missing, generates an intra-crate deps SVG with `cargo deps --no-deps --format svg --manifest-path <crate>/Cargo.toml > target/deps-graph.svg`, and exits non-zero on failure. Strict mode + set -euo pipefail + trap.
Added `cargo-deps-graph` job between fmt and coverage. 4 steps: checkout (pinned SHA), rust-toolchain stable, run script with `pheno-errors` arg, upload SVG as artifact `pheno-errors-deps-graph`.
Same job inserted between fmt and coverage. Uses floating @v4 tags (matches existing convention in this file) for upload-artifact.
Same job inserted between msrv and coverage. Uses pinned SHA + comments matching the existing SHA-pin convention in this file.

observations.scope_reality_vs_plan: Plan called for 11 fleet crates. After `git ls-tree origin/main` + `find -name Cargo.toml` on origin/main, only 3 Rust crates exist in argis-extensions today (pheno-errors, pheno-otel, pheno-port-adapter). The remaining 8 names in the plan (pheno-flags, pheno-cli-base, pheno-pydantic-models, pheno-llms-txt, pheno-mcp-router, etc.) have no Cargo.toml on origin/main — they exist only as pheno-* Go subdirectories without Cargo manifests, or live in other repos. Therefore T1 was scoped down to the 3 Rust crates that actually have a Cargo.toml on origin/main. Full plan fulfillment requires the other crates to be Rust-ified in a later wave.

yaml_validation.method: ruby -ryaml -e YAML.load_file(f) on all 3 modified ci.yml files

pillar_impact.rationale: L3 (deps graph) measures whether each Rust crate has a machine-generated dependency graph artifact in CI. Before: 0/3 crates had any deps graph. After: 3/3 have a CI job that emits + uploads an SVG. Score moves from 1 (no graph) to 3 (CI-generated, artifact-uploaded).

git_workflow.parallel_branch_untouched: feat/v12-08-figment-cascade-2026-06-20 (orchestrator's working branch — left untouched as required)

next_wave_hooks.v14_T2_track: chore/v14-T2-cargo-config-2026-06-20 already on origin — no overlap
next_wave_hooks.v14_T3_track: chore/v14-T3-perf-budget-2026-06-20 already on origin — no overlap
next_wave_hooks.v14_T4_plus: If more Rust crates land on main (the other 8 pheno-* names), T1 must be re-opened to add the CI job to each new crate. Track via `git ls-tree origin/main | grep Cargo.toml` as the source of truth.
next_wave_hooks.blockers: None — script + 3 CI jobs merged in single commit 833291c. cargo-deps binary install (~2-3 min) is the dominant CI cost; budgeted via 15-min timeout.
